subject: Careers
topic: Paper airplane prototype progression and improvement checklist

teacher_guide.title: Careers 8 Teacher Guide - Paper Airplane Prototype Challenge
teacher_guide.overview: Two-day prototype challenge focused on evidence-based improvement, teamwork, safety, and career skill reflection.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How do people improve a product or process by using evidence, feedback, and perseverance?
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Students work like a product design team: prototype, test, improve, and explain the evidence.

timing.activity: Build and test Prototype 0, Prototype 1, and Prototype 2. Complete the exit ticket.
Use Monday evidence for one final improvement.
Official flights, final reflection, and career skill connection.

teacher_notes: Assess evidence, improvement, safe process, and explanation, not only distance.
Stop random rebuilding by asking: What changed from the last version?
Use the tracker as the centre of the lesson.
Extending means transfer beyond this airplane task, not extra neatness.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Design, test, improve, and officially fly a paper airplane prototype.
matching_bank: Twists or dives: check symmetry, nose tightness, wing tips, and balance.
Short flight: try sharper folds, narrower wings, one small weight, or consistent force.
Off course: check wing angles, bent folds, wing tip adjustment, or launch angle.

model.title: Teacher model
model.sample: Prototype 1 flew straighter because we made the wings more even. Our next test will keep that change and adjust nose weight.

teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Prototype progression, evidence-based improvement, safe team process, and career skill transfer.

task_sheet.title: Careers 8 Student Task - Paper Airplane Prototype Challenge
instructions: Tuesday: final work time and official flights.
Change one main thing at a time.

tasks.label: Team roles
tasks.prompt: Set roles: designer, test pilot, data recorder, quality checker.

tasks.label: Prototype 0
tasks.prompt: Build your baseline and record the first result.

tasks.label: Prototype 1
tasks.prompt: Change one feature for stability and record evidence.

tasks.label: Prototype 2
tasks.prompt: Change one feature for distance or accuracy and record evidence.

embedded_supports: One change that helped was ___ because ___.
success_criteria: I tested more than one prototype.
I changed one thing at a time.
I connected the work to a career skill.

graphic_organizer.title: Prototype Progression Tracker
graphic_organizer.prompt: Record evidence after each test so decisions are based on proof.
success_criteria: I recorded a change and result for each prototype.

station_cards.title: Prototype Improvement Cards
instructions: Choose one change before testing again.
Record what happened before choosing another card.

cards.title: Stability
cards.task: Check symmetry, nose tightness, wing tips, or balance. Choose one.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should name the change and whether flight control improved.

cards.title: Distance
cards.prompt: Plane flies straight but not far.
cards.task: Try sharper folds, narrower wings, one small weight, or consistent launch force. Choose one.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should compare distance before and after.

cards.title: Accuracy
cards.task: Check wing angles, bent folds, one wing tip, or launch angle. Choose one.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should describe straighter flight or target improvement.

cards.title: Team process
cards.task: Return to data and choose the most testable next change.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should connect the decision to a prior result.

rubric_sheet.title: Paper Airplane Prototype Challenge Rubric
rubric_sheet.purpose_line: Each box explains how to reach the level. Extending means transfer beyond this assigned task.

level_descriptors.1: Build one version or make random changes. To improve: label each version and record one change.
level_descriptors.2: Build more than one version, but changes are unclear. To improve: change one main feature before each test.
level_descriptors.3: Build multiple versions and change one feature at a time using the tracker.
level_descriptors.4: Transfer the cycle by explaining how it could improve another product, routine, or workplace problem.

level_descriptors.1: Record little evidence. To improve: write one distance, score, or observation after each test.
level_descriptors.2: Record some results but do not always use them. To improve: connect the next change to a previous result.
level_descriptors.3: Use evidence to choose and justify the final Tuesday improvement.
level_descriptors.4: Identify a pattern, limitation, or fair-test issue and explain how better data would improve the process.

level_descriptors.1: Name a skill with little explanation. To improve: give one example from the work.
level_descriptors.2: Explain a skill simply. To improve: connect it to a team decision or challenge.
level_descriptors.3: Explain how teamwork, communication, accuracy, safety, patience, or project management affected the result.
level_descriptors.4: Explain how this skill matters in a real job, workplace team, or future project with different constraints.

success_criteria: Extending shows transfer to a new context.

exit_ticket.title: Monday Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: Our best prototype was ___. Evidence: ___. On Tuesday, we will change ___ because ___.
success_criteria: I used evidence and planned one Tuesday improvement.

tuesday_official_run_sheet.title: Tuesday Official Run Sheet
instructions: Use this after final work time.

tasks.label: Final change
tasks.prompt: What final change did you make today, and why?

tasks.label: Official launches
tasks.prompt: Record Launch 1, Launch 2, and your best result.

tasks.label: Evidence statement
tasks.prompt: What did the official run prove about your design?

final_reflection_sheet.title: Final Reflection - Prototype Thinking and Career Skills
final_reflection_sheet.prompt: Explain how your team improved the prototype and what career skill helped most. Use evidence. For Extending, connect this process to a different product, job, or project.
success_criteria: I connected to a career skill.

reduced_load_student_version.title: Reduced-Load Version

tasks.label: Round 1
tasks.prompt: Choose: wings more even OR nose tighter.

tasks.label: Round 2
tasks.prompt: Choose: add one paper clip OR remove one paper clip.

tasks.label: Round 3
tasks.prompt: Choose: change launch angle OR adjust wing tips.

tasks.label: Final
tasks.prompt: Our best plane was ___ because ___. One career skill I used was ___ because ___.

embedded_supports: Better means farther, straighter, or more consistent.
success_criteria: I made one choice per round.

teacher_observation_checklist.title: Teacher Observation Checklist
teacher_observation_checklist.purpose_line: Use while circulating. Mark evidence and quick feedback.

criteria.name: One-change testing
criteria.descriptor: Students test one change at a time.

level_descriptors.2: Can name a change after prompting.
level_descriptors.3: Names the change before testing and records the result.
level_descriptors.4: Explains why isolating one variable matters in other contexts.

level_descriptors.2: Evidence recorded but not driving decisions.
level_descriptors.4: Identifies a pattern, limitation, or better data method.

level_descriptors.1: Unsafe or off-task behaviour interrupts testing.
level_descriptors.3: Uses roles and launch routines independently.
level_descriptors.4: Improves team process or supports another group without taking over.

content.subtitle: Build. Test. Improve. Explain the evidence.

rows: Work like a product design team.
Then explain how the process transfers to another product, job, or team problem.
